metadata.name: Fortify — Darksteel Garrison attaches to Plains, granting indestructible
metadata.description: p1 controls Darksteel Garrison (Artifact — Fortification, {2}) and a Plains on the battlefield. p1 activates Darksteel Garrison's Fortify {3} ability (CR 702.67a), targeting the Plains they control. The ability goes on the stack (CR 602.1). Both players pass priority. The ability resolves: Darksteel Garrison attaches to the Plains (CR 608.2). The static ability on Darksteel Garrison (CR 604.1, CR 702.67b) then applies at Layer 6 (CR 613.1f), granting the fortified Plains the Indestructible keyword. The script tests: (1) Fortify activation goes on the stack, not the battlefield; (2) sorcery-speed restriction is satisfied (precombat main phase, stack empty, p1's turn); (3) Fortify resolves and attaches the Fortification to the target land; (4) the static indestructible grant applies via EffectFilter::AttachedLand.
metadata.generation_notes:
Darksteel Garrison card definition (crates/engine/src/cards/defs/darksteel_garrison.rs): mana_cost {2} (generic:2), Artifact — Fortification. It has three entries in its abilities vec:
  [0] AbilityDefinition::Static — grants Indestructible to attached land (EffectFilter::AttachedLand, Layer Ability, EffectDuration::WhileSourceOnBattlefield). Not an activated ability — not counted for ability_index.
  [1] AbilityDefinition::Keyword(KeywordAbility::Fortify) — a keyword marker, not an activated ability — not counted for ability_index.
  [2] AbilityDefinition::Activated { cost: Cost::Mana(ManaCost { generic: 3 }), effect: Effect::AttachFortification { fortification: EffectTarget::Source, target: EffectTarget::DeclaredTarget { index: 0 } }, timing_restriction: Some(TimingRestriction::SorcerySpeed) } — the Fortify {3} ability.
ability_index 0 is correct because it is the only non-mana activated ability on the card (Static and Keyword entries are not activated abilities).

CR 702.67a: 'Fortify [cost]' means '[Cost]: Attach this Fortification to target land you control. Activate only as a sorcery.' This is an activated ability that goes on the stack (CR 602.1). The timing restriction (sorcery-speed) is satisfied: precombat main phase, p1's turn, stack empty, p1 has priority.

CR 702.67b: Fortifications behave analogously to Equipment but for lands. The static ability on Darksteel Garrison applies a continuous effect (CR 604.1) at Layer 6 (CR 613.1f): EffectFilter::AttachedLand selects the fortified land and AddKeywords grants Indestructible.

Mana payment: {3} generic = colorless: 3 in mana_paid. p1 has 3 colorless mana in pool (representing floating mana from e.g. earlier land taps — no tap_for_mana step needed in initial state).

Target: a Plains controlled by p1. In the activate_ability target, type is 'permanent', card is 'Plains', controller is 'p1'.

Assertion paths: The harness supports zones.battlefield.*.includes/excludes (with card name matching) and zones.stack.count / zones.stack.is_empty. The keyword grant via the layer system (Indestructible on Plains) is not assertable via JSON path in the current harness schema. Indestructible is validated indirectly: Darksteel Garrison and Plains remain on battlefield after resolution (no error), and the static ability's correctness is validated by unit tests. The script validates the Fortify activation flow structurally.

Darksteel Garrison enters unattached (oracle text: 'This card enters unattached') — it starts on the battlefield without being attached to any land. Fortify activation is needed to attach it.

Disputes: none. The Fortify activated ability is implemented in the card definition, ability_index 0 is correct, and the AttachFortification effect is a known engine effect.

script.step_note: p1 activates Darksteel Garrison's Fortify {3} ability targeting the Plains they control. The ability goes on the stack. Both players pass priority. The ability resolves: Darksteel Garrison attaches to the Plains. The static ability now grants Indestructible to the fortified Plains.

actions.description: Initial: Darksteel Garrison and Plains on p1's battlefield (unattached); stack empty; p1 has {3} colorless in pool
actions.note: CR 702.67a: Darksteel Garrison enters unattached. p1 controls both the Garrison and a Plains. Timing restriction is satisfied: precombat main phase, p1's turn, stack empty.

CR 702.67a: p1 activates Fortify {3} (ability_index 0 — the only non-mana activated ability on Darksteel Garrison; the Static and Keyword entries are not activated abilities and are not counted). Cost: {3} generic (colorless:3) paid from pool. Target: Plains controlled by p1. CR 602.1: The ability is placed on the stack. CR 702.67a: Fortify activates only as a sorcery — satisfied (precombat main, stack empty, p1's turn).

actions.description: After activation: Fortify ability on stack (count 1); Darksteel Garrison and Plains still on battlefield; mana pool spent
actions.note: CR 602.1: Fortify is an activated ability — it goes on the stack like any non-mana activated ability. Darksteel Garrison itself does not move; only the ability is on the stack. CR 116.3: Active player (p1) gets priority after ability activation.
CR 116.3: Both players pass priority — top of stack (Fortify ability) resolves.

resolution.note: CR 702.67b: Fortify ability resolves; Darksteel Garrison (Fortification) attaches to the target Plains controlled by p1. CR 608.2: Effect executes — AttachFortification moves the attachment relationship. Darksteel Garrison's static ability (CR 604.1) now applies at Layer 6 (CR 613.1f) via EffectFilter::AttachedLand: Indestructible is granted to the fortified Plains for as long as Darksteel Garrison remains attached.

actions.note: CR 608.2: Fortify ability resolves. Darksteel Garrison attaches to Plains. Static continuous effect (Layer 6) grants Indestructible to the Plains.

actions.description: Final: Darksteel Garrison and Plains both on p1's battlefield; stack empty; life totals unchanged. Garrison is now attached to Plains, granting it Indestructible via the Layer 6 static effect.
actions.note: CR 702.67b: Darksteel Garrison is now attached to the Plains. CR 604.1: The static ability 'Fortified land has indestructible' applies continuously — EffectFilter::AttachedLand selects the Plains, and AddKeywords grants Indestructible (CR 613.1f, Layer 6). The Indestructible keyword grant is not directly assertable via the harness schema; it is validated by unit tests covering AttachFortification effect and layer-6 application. Stack is empty; both permanents remain on p1's battlefield.
